title: ChromeDevTools/chrome-devtools-mcp
friendlyTitle: Chrome DevTools MCP lets AI agents inspect Chrome
summary: Chrome DevTools MCP lets coding agents control and inspect a live Chrome browser through the Model Context Protocol. It exposes browser automation, console logs, network inspection, screenshots, performance tracing, and debugging tools to compatible agents.
friendlySummary: This repo connects AI coding tools to Chrome DevTools so they can look at pages, logs, network requests, and performance data.
whyItMatters: It gives coding assistants a practical way to debug real web pages instead of guessing from source files alone. Teams can use it for browser-based testing, performance checks, and agent-assisted frontend debugging, while being careful with sensitive browser data.
friendlyWhyItMatters: Developers can use it to help AI assistants debug websites more accurately.
howItsUseful: It gives coding assistants a practical way to debug real web pages instead of guessing from source files alone. Teams can use it for browser-based testing, performance checks, and agent-assisted frontend debugging, while being careful with sensitive browser data.
friendlyHowItsUseful: Developers can use it to help AI assistants debug websites more accurately.
